pi_items.source: Task scripts and WooCommerce documentation confirm this is the required API key description. setup_task.sh and export_result.sh query for keys with this exact description. This is a user-defined field with no standard reference; the value is correct as specified in the task requirements.

WooCommerce REST API documentation and database schema (https://github.com/woocommerce/woocommerce/wiki/Database-Description) confirm that the wp_woocommerce_api_keys table stores permissions as varchar(10) with values 'read', 'write', or 'read_write' in lowercase with underscore. The export_result.sh script (line 58) checks for perms == 'read_write'. The verifier.py script (line 58) also validates this exact value.

The task.json description (line 5) specifies saving credentials to '/home/ga/api_credentials.txt'. The export_result.sh script (line 46) hardcodes this exact path as FILE_PATH=/home/ga/api_credentials.txt. This path is part of the Docker-based test environment where user 'ga' has home directory at /home/ga.

WooCommerce REST API v3 documentation (https://woocommerce.github.io/woocommerce-rest-api-docs/v3.html) confirms the system_status endpoint exists at /wp-json/wc/v3/system_status. The export_result.sh script (line 93) tests exactly this endpoint using curl with basic authentication. The endpoint supports read-only access via GET and requires API key authentication.

privileged_info_summary: The task generates WooCommerce REST API keys with the description 'ShipStation Integration' and expects read_write permissions. Credentials are saved to /home/ga/api_credentials.txt. The generated keys are tested against the WooCommerce v3 REST API system_status endpoint (http://localhost/wp-json/wc/v3/system_status) using basic authentication with the consumer key and consumer secret. All metadata values align with WooCommerce's actual API implementation and database schema.
